newsSince: Material news since June 9: on June 16 Mobileye announced a strategic pivot to a vertically integrated robotaxi business it will own/operate (an initial ~100-vehicle US fleet in 2027, scaling toward ~17,000). This is a capital-intensive AV-operations shift, distinct from the Mentee humanoid/physical-AI thesis the robotics rank was built on, and lands amid a hardening competitive backdrop (Stellantis-Wayve-Uber L4 MOU, China robotaxi dominance) that reinforces the earlier Jefferies Underperform/$8-target case. Stock -13.2% since June 9 to ~$8.10.

sources.label: Mobileye to establish vertically integrated robotaxi business (June 16)
Mobileye US robotaxi launch puts it on both sides of AV business (TechCrunch, June 16)
Mobileye to establish vertically integrated robotaxi business (Jun 16)
MBLY tumbles on Jefferies Underperform / $8 PT
Mobileye stock hit as robotaxi rivals turn up heat

verdicts.rankRationale: Humanoid-stack lens. June 16 pivot to OWN/operate robotaxi fleets is capital-heavy, execution-risky and dilutes focus from the asset-light Mentee physical-AI/humanoid thesis this listing rests on. Jefferies Underperform/$8 PT, ~$1.35B cash vs ~$850M+ fleet capex. June 9 moved it UP; reverse to down.
verdicts.bull: Mentee humanoid stack + AD IP still real physical-AI optionality if robotaxi economics work.
verdicts.neutral: Strategic bet could justify premium but is years out and unproven.
verdicts.bear: Late, cash-light robotaxi operator; capital intensity dilutes humanoid focus; $40k+$0.20/mi model unproven.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Capital depletion, fleet-economics failure, robotaxi competition
